title: A republic is born
content: Rome began as one city among many in Italy. After casting out its kings, it built a republic — a system of elected officials and shared power that would last for centuries.
next: How did one city grow so powerful?

title: Clash with Carthage
content: Across the sea, the wealthy city of Carthage controlled trade and territory. A series of wars between the two powers would decide who ruled the western Mediterranean.

title: Master of the Mediterranean
content: Victory over Carthage and expansion into the Greek world left Rome without a serious rival. The sea the Romans called 'our sea' was now ringed by Roman power.

title: From republic to empire
content: Decades of internal conflict ended when a single ruler took control. Rome was no longer a republic — it had become an empire.
